stemHtml:
A valley inversion is formed by:

1. Katabatic winds drawing cold air into the valley
2. Anabatic winds sending warm air up the valley and forcing cold air into the valley
3. Air in valley is unstable so inversion dissipates quickly and has no effect on aviation
4. Air in valley is stable; visibility reduced below inversion. Downdraughts are also present
5. Clouds above the mountain stop the top of a mountain cooling
6. Cold air settles in low-lying areas during the night, trapping warmer air above
